name: Google Play Developer API
what_it_does: Google's REST API for Android app management on Google Play — enabling Android developers to automate APK/AAB uploads, manage releases across tracks (internal, alpha, beta, production), update app metadata, manage in-app purchases and subscriptions, access sales and statistics data, and respond to user reviews, with OAuth2 authentication and service account support for CI/CD automation.
use_cases: Automating Android app builds via Google Play API for CI/CD upload and release pipelines
Managing release tracks via Google Play API for staged rollout and phased releases
Fetching app sales and statistics via Google Play API for revenue and analytics dashboards
Managing in-app products and subscriptions via Google Play API for billing automation
Responding to user reviews via Google Play API for automated review management
not_for: iOS app publishing (use App Store Connect API for Apple platforms)
End-user in-app purchases (use Google Play Billing Library in app code for in-app purchases)
Real-time crash analytics (use Firebase Crashlytics for detailed crash reporting)
best_when: An Android development team needs to automate Google Play management — CI/CD integration, release track management, metadata updates, and analytics reporting without manual Play Console access.
avoid_when: You don't publish Android apps or need in-app runtime functionality (different APIs/SDKs).

auth.notes: OAuth2 via Google Identity Platform. Service account recommended for CI/CD (no user interaction). Service account JSON key file. Scope: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/androidpublisher. OAuth2 access token in Authorization: Bearer header. 1-hour token expiry for service accounts (auto-refreshed by Google client libraries). Developer account must grant service account access in Play Console. Google API client libraries simplify auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free with Google Play developer account ($25 one-time registration)
pricing.paid_tiers: No subscription; Google Play: 15% commission on first $1M revenue, 30% thereafter
pricing.notes: Google Inc. (Mountain View, CA). $25 one-time Play developer registration fee. API: android-publisher:v3. Google APIs client libraries in 6 languages. Quota: 1,000 product requests/day, unlimited edits. Edits API: create edit → modify → commit (transactional). Release tracks: internal, alpha, beta, production. Staged rollout: percentage-based production rollout. App bundles (AAB) recommended over APK. Subscriptions: management via Subscription API. Reviews: list and reply via Reviews API. Reporting: aggregated statistics via Cloud Storage export.

performance.rate_limits: 1,000 requests/day for most resources; 200,000/day for edits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OAuth2 service account for CI/CD. Edits API (transactional). 1,000 req/day quota for products. Service account JSON key. Google API client libraries. $25 one-time developer fee.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Edits API is transactional — create edit → modify resources → commit. Read operations idempotent. Edit commits are idempotent (same edit ID). Subscription reads idempotent by purchaseToken.
known_agent_gotchas: Edits API is transactional: create edit (POST /edits) → modify → commit (POST /edits/{editId}:commit)
Service account must be granted access in Play Console (Account > Users and Permissions)
APK/AAB upload must happen within same edit before commit — separate edits required for separate uploads
1,000 requests/day quota for 'product' resources — check quota for high-volume subscription management
Staged rollout: set userFraction (0.0-1.0) for percentage-based production rollout
Service account JSON key is a high-value secret — store in secret manager, not source code

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 service account (recommended for CI/CD — no user credentials in pipeline). Single scope: androidpublisher — broad access. Service account JSON key: high-value secret requiring secure storage. Google infrastructure security (SOC2/ISO27001). App and sales data is sensitive business IP. 1-hour auto-refreshed tokens. Google API client libraries handle token refresh securely.
